page: The UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-4GB is an x86_64 architecture virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to AMD EPYC 9575F physical processors running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 30 GB local SSD storage volume and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, single-threaded gzip compression at 41.86 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with read-only large block speeds at 68.79 GB/sec and write speeds at 50.39 GB/sec.

A key tradeoff lies in the memory and database performance. The PassMark memory score and database operations benchmarks both rank in the bottom 25%, indicating a bottleneck for memory-intensive or heavy transactional database workloads. This is further influenced by the shared CPU allocation model. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a highly economical entry point, delivering strong single-thread compute capabilities relative to its resource tier, though it is constrained by its 4.0 GB memory capacity.

This configuration is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale applications. It is also appropriate for static web serving, which achieved an average throughput of 9.62 GB/sec, and lightweight Redis deployments, processing 6.66 million SET operations per second. It is not recommended for memory-heavy applications or production-grade databases.
description: The UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-4GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and AMD EPYC 9575F processors. It features 2 shared vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 30 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks show a stark contrast: single-core CPU performance is in the top 10%, highlighted by strong single-threaded gzip compression and stress-ng results. Conversely, memory performance and database operations are weak, falling into the bottom 25% of benchmarks. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput remain average. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for entry-level workloads, but the shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity present clear tradeoffs. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and single-threaded utility tasks rather than memory-intensive database applications.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-4GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4GB RAM, and 30GB SSD. It offers top-tier single-core speed but exhibits weak memory and database performance.
meta_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-4GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4GB RAM, and 30GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared virtual server delivering strong single-threaded compute performance alongside bundled SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 30 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weak memory and database operations performance ranking in the bottom 25%
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
